response: Ckm wz. 25 Hotchkiss was a Polish derivative of the Hotchkiss M1914 machine gun, which became the standard machine gun of the French Army during World War I. The Hotchkiss M1914, manufactured by the French arms company Hotchkiss et Cie, was gas-actuated and chambered for the 8mm Lebel cartridge, making it the primary machine gun of the French Army during World War I.
